finalOutput.content:
The recommended defaults for `RagPipeline` are chunk size 800, top-K 5, and a short plain-English synthesis prompt. [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #0] [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #1] [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #3]

These defaults are justified by the evaluation lessons that 500 chunk size produced too many tiny chunks, 1200 lost recall on dense PDFs, and 800 was peer-aligned; K=10 added prompt tokens without proportional recall gain, so top-K 5 was chosen; and a short plain-English prompt beat enumerated multi-rule prompts for small models, dropping refusal rate from 36 percent to 12 percent with no hallucination regression. [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #1] [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #3]

steps.question: What are the recommended defaults for chunk size, top-K, and the synthesis prompt when building a RAG pipeline with `RagPipeline`, and which lessons from the IntelliDoc 2026-04-26 evaluation justify t…

steps.content:
The recommended defaults for `RagPipeline` are chunk size 800, top-K 5, and a short plain-English synthesis prompt. [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #0] [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #1] [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #3]

These defaults are justified by the evaluation lessons that 500 chunk size produced too many tiny chunks, 1200 lost recall on dense PDFs, and 800 was peer-aligned; K=10 added prompt tokens without proportional recall gain, so top-K 5 was chosen; and a short plain-English prompt beat enumerated multi-rule prompts for small models, dropping refusal rate from 36 percent to 12 percent with no hallucination regression. [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #1] [source: RAG_LESSONS.md #3]
